item.title: Gilead Sciences Deal Strategy
item.description:
• Summary: Gilead Sciences is emphasizing a strategic shift towards fewer large M&A deals, instead focusing on partnerships and bolt-on acquisitions to expand its oncology and autoimmune pipelines. Recent deals with companies like Arcellx and Ouro highlight this approach, as Gilead aims to diversify beyond its HIV franchise.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: BioPharma Dive, BioSpace, Endpoints News

item.title: Novavax Corporate Governance
item.description:
• Summary: Novavax faces corporate governance challenges as shareholder Shah Capital pushes for changes, including opposition to the CEO's pay package and calls for new leadership. The activist investor has sent a letter to the board ahead of a shareholder vote, highlighting ongoing struggles post-COVID-19 vaccine market shifts.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: BioPharma Dive, BioSpace

item.title: Sidewinder Therapeutics (Bispecific ADCs)
item.description:
• Summary: Sidewinder Therapeutics has secured $137 million in financing led by OrbiMed to advance its platform of bispecific antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs). The funding will support the development of novel cancer therapies that combine tumor targeting with payload delivery, aiming to improve efficacy and reduce toxicity.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: BioPharma Dive, Endpoints News

item.title: Terns Pharmaceuticals / Merck Deal
item.description:
• Summary: Merck has reduced its offer to Terns Pharmaceuticals by $1 billion after reviewing updated leukemia data for Terns' drug candidate, likely TERN-701 for chronic myeloid leukemia. The revised deal terms reflect concerns about the drug's competitiveness in a crowded market, impacting the potential partnership.
  • MoAs: TERN-701 is a BCR-ABL tyrosine kinase inhibitor targeting the BCR-ABL fusion protein in chronic myeloid leukemia.
  • Sources: STAT News, BioSpace

item.title: Amgen CEO Pay
item.description:
• Summary: Amgen CEO Robert Bradway's compensation for 2025 charted a slight increase to $24.7 million, following a significant bump in the previous year, reflecting ongoing corporate governance discussions.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Fierce Pharma

item.title: Merck Vaccine Supply Deal in China
item.description:
• Summary: Merck has adjusted its vaccine supply agreement in China, concurrently with Soleno Therapeutics withdrawing a European Union application, highlighting strategic shifts in global vaccine distribution and regulatory engagements.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Endpoints News

item.title: Novo Nordisk Wegovy Pricing
item.description:
• Summary: Novo Nordisk is navigating pricing strategies for its weight-loss drug Wegovy (semaglutide), amid market competition and payer dynamics, with implications for accessibility and revenue in the obesity therapeutics space.
  • MoAs: Wegovy (semaglutide) is a GLP-1 receptor agonist that mimics incretin hormones to stimulate insulin secretion, suppress glucagon, slow gastric emptying, and promote satiety, leading to improved glycemic control and weight loss.
  • Sources: BioPharma Dive

item.title: Pfizer Remote Work / Office Space
item.description:
• Summary: Pfizer is transitioning employees to remote work and vacating underutilized office space in San Francisco, part of a broader corporate real estate optimization and flexible work policy adaptation.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Fierce Pharma

item.title: Cognito Therapeutics Partnership
item.description:
• Summary: Cognito Therapeutics has partnered with a nonprofit organization to remove the term 'dementia' from its communications and scientific materials, focusing on non-pharmacological neuromodulation approaches for cognitive disorders.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Fierce Pharma

item.title: Talkspace & Universal Health Services Deal
item.description:
• Summary: The deal between Talkspace and Universal Health Services integrates teletherapy services into hospital systems, expanding access to mental health care through digital health partnerships.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Endpoints News

item.title: Invivyd Marketing Campaign
item.description:
• Summary: Invivyd launched the 'Antibodies for Any Body' marketing campaign featuring Olympic skier Lindsey Vonn to promote its monoclonal antibody therapies for infectious diseases, emphasizing broad patient applicability.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Fierce Pharma

item.title: Dare Bioscience & Women's Health
item.description:
• Summary: Dare Bioscience's CEO is advocating for increased investment in women's health, starting with her company. The article highlights challenges in funding for women's health startups and emphasizes the need for more resources in this underserved area, reflecting broader corporate strategy to address healthcare gaps.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Biospace
